Where the data points to opportunity
This pack is designed to drive brainstorming grounded in evidence.

We map Barclays’ own FY25 data and peer signals to the bank’s stated strategy:
Simpler. Better. More balanced.

The intent is not to prescribe solutions, but to surface where focus matters most, what peers appear to be doing differently, and how closing those gaps could translate into measurable bottom‑line outcomes.
Source: Barclays FY25 Results & Targets Update; peer benchmark pack
Barclays’ stated ambition
Simpler
Reduce cost and complexity through standardisation, modernisation and operational resilience.
Better
Improve returns via stronger capital productivity, segment performance and customer outcomes.
More balanced
Build a more resilient earnings mix across lending, fees and geographies.
Simpler: cost efficiency trajectory

61% 2026
~58% 2028
~52%
The plan assumes c.£2bn of efficiency savings by 2028. The glidepath implies diminishing headroom from incremental savings — structural simplification becomes decisive.
Better: RoTE ambition

11.3% 2026
>12% 2028
>14%
“Better” is framed less as growth, more as capital productivity and predictability.
Peer lens: valuation signal

~1.0x Peers
>1.2x
Peer data shows Barclays trading around book value, while simpler peers trade at a premium — suggesting the market is not yet convinced returns are durable.
More balanced: income mix focus
The strategy explicitly shifts toward stable NII, fees and disciplined UK lending growth (>5% CAGR).
From focus to financial outcome
Do X (simplify platforms, improve RWA productivity, scale fee engines)
→ Move Y (cost:income, RoTE, volatility, capital efficiency)
→ Improve Z (earnings quality, investor confidence, valuation multiple)
What this enables
This framing allows senior stakeholders to:
  • Anchor debate in Barclays’ own data
  • Use peers as a reference, not a prescription
  • Brainstorm interventions with a clear line to outcomes

The question becomes: where will focus most accelerate a Simpler, Better, More balanced Barclays?
``
